questions.q: From the following incomplete records of A, B, and C, prepare a trading account and profit and loss account for the year ending 31st March, 2022.
questions.topic: Preparation of Trading and Profit and Loss Account from Incomplete Records
questions.answer:
Trading Account:
Date
Particulars
2022
To Opening Stock : 15,000
By Purchases : 90,000
By Closing Stock : 18,000

Profit and Loss Account:
Date
Particulars
2022
To Opening Capital : 40,000
By Balance b/d : 42,000
By Profit : 12,000

Explanation: Opening stock was given as 15,000. Purchases were 90,000. Closing stock was 18,000. Opening capital was 40,000. Balance brought down was 42,000. Profit was 12,000. The trading account shows the difference between purchases and sales, i.e., cost of goods sold. Profit and Loss account shows the net income from the business and the drawings of the proprietor.
marking_scheme: Proper opening stock, purchases, and closing stock values (1 mark)
Proper opening capital, balance brought down, and profit values (2 marks)

questions.q: From the following data of X Ltd., prepare a trading account, profit and loss account, and the balance sheet for the year ending 31st December, 2022.
questions.topic: Preparation of Trading and Profit and Loss Account and Balance Sheet from Incomplete Records
questions.answer:
Trading Account:
Date
Particulars
2022
To Sales : 2,50,000
By Opening Stock : 20,000
By Purchases : 1,80,000
By Closing Stock : 22,000

Profit and Loss Account:
Date
Particulars
2022
To Opening Capital : 1,00,000
By Balance b/d : 1,22,000
By Profit : 28,000

Balance Sheet:
Date
Particulars
2022
To Share Capital : 1,00,000
By Profit : 28,000
By Balance b/d : 1,22,000

Explanation: Sales were 2,50,000. Opening stock was 20,000. Purchases were 1,80,000. Closing stock was 22,000. Opening capital was 1,00,000. Balance brought down was 1,22,000. Profit was 28,000. The trading account shows the difference between sales and cost of goods sold. Profit and Loss account shows the net income from the business and the drawings of the proprietor. Balance sheet shows the total assets and capital of the company.
marking_scheme: Proper sales, opening stock, purchases, and closing stock values (1 mark)
Proper opening capital, balance brought down, and profit values (2 marks)
